questions.question: How many seats are authorized on the board or committee, how many are currently filled, and how many vacancies exist?
questions.answer: The Ecology and Natural Resources Advisory Committee (ENRAC) is authorized to have up to 18 members, including a Chair, all appointed by the City Manager (§ 9-99, amended 5-17-2022). Additionally, up to 3 Auxiliary Youth Members may be appointed by the City Manager under § 9-99.1 (added 1-21-2025). The number of currently filled seats and any existing vacancies are not specified in the provided sources.
questions.gap: The statute specifies the maximum authorized seats (up to 18 regular members plus 3 auxiliary youth members) but does not provide current filled seats or vacancy counts. Full alignment with best practice would require a current roster or vacancy listing.
nextPrompts: Where can the current roster of appointed ENRAC members be found on the City of New Rochelle's official website?
Are there any public records or City Manager reports listing current ENRAC vacancies as of 2025?

questions.question: Does the website clearly list member names, appointment method, term lengths, and expiration dates?
questions.answer: The statute specifies that up to 18 members (including a Chair) are appointed by and serve at the pleasure of the City Manager (§9-99). Three Auxiliary Youth Members may also be appointed by the City Manager, must be New Rochelle residents under age 20, require endorsements from the New Rochelle City School District Green Schools Committee and ENRAC, serve one-year terms, and are selected through a competitive application process (§9-99.1). No specific term lengths or expiration dates are stated for the regular 18 members beyond serving 'at the pleasure of the City Manager.' Individual member names are not listed in the statute.
questions.gap: The statute does not list individual member names, term lengths for regular members, or term expiration dates. Only auxiliary youth members have defined one-year terms. This falls short of the best practice of clearly listing member names, appointment method, term lengths, and expiration dates for all members.
nextPrompts: Does the City of New Rochelle maintain a public roster of current ENRAC members with appointment dates and term expirations on its website?
Are there any administrative rules or resolutions that establish fixed term lengths for the 18 regular ENRAC members?

questions.question: Does the board or committee page show ongoing projects, priorities, or current work in progress?
questions.answer: The statute establishes the Ecology and Natural Resources Advisory Committee (ENRAC) and designates it as the City's official Climate Smart Community Task Force (Resolution No. 42-2022). The Sustainability Coordinator is designated as the Climate Smart Community Coordinator. A 2025 resolution (Res. No. 2025-14) authorized the addition of three Auxiliary Youth Members, indicating recent organizational activity. However, no specific ongoing projects, priorities, or current work in progress are described in the provided statute text.
questions.gap: The statute does not include links or references to ongoing projects, priorities, or current work in progress. Best practice calls for a clearly accessible link to active initiatives and current work, which is absent from the provided sources.
nextPrompts: Does the City of New Rochelle's website provide a dedicated ENRAC page listing current projects or priorities?
What specific initiatives has ENRAC undertaken as the Climate Smart Community Task Force?
